title: Neonatal Respiratory Therapy
authors: David J. Durand & Sherry E. Courtney

blocks.text:
ทำไม neonatologist ต้องเข้าใจ Neonatal Respiratory Therapy อย่างลึก?
Respiratory therapy = the most life-critical intervention ใน NICU. สิ่งสำคัญ: (1) mPaw (mean airway pressure) คือ single number ที่บอก 'ระดับ support' ที่ให้ — เพิ่ม mPaw = ↑ FRC = ↑ oxygenation; (2) CPAP-first strategy ลด BPD และ intubation — SUPPORT/COIN trials; (3) Volume-targeted ventilation (VTV) ดีกว่า pressure control ใน RDS (ลด BPD + IVH); (4) Surfactant = cornerstone ของ RDS treatment; INSURE / LISA / MIST เป็น minimal-invasive strategies; (5) HFOV + HFJV ใช้ใน refractory cases — mechanism ต่างกัน; (6) SpO2 target 91–95% ใน preterm — ลด ROP โดยไม่เพิ่ม mortality (BoOST/SUPPORT/COT).
เป้าหมายการเรียนรู้วันนี้
อธิบาย spectrum ของ respiratory support: NC → HFNC → NCPAP → NIV → conventional MV → HFV
อธิบาย mPaw เป็น key parameter และวิธีคำนวณจาก ventilator settings
รู้ ventilator modes: SIMV, AC/VG, PC-SIMV, PRVC — เปรียบเทียบ VTV vs pressure control
อธิบาย surfactant therapy: indications, preparation, administration (ET, LISA, MIST), การ monitor
อธิบาย HFOV: parameters (mPaw, frequency, amplitude/ΔP), mechanism, indications, troubleshooting
อธิบาย HFJV: ต่างจาก HFOV อย่างไร; parameters, indications (PIE, MAS, CDH)
รู้ SpO2 target ใน preterm: evidence, acceptable range, alarm limits
วางแผน weaning strategy: extubation readiness criteria, post-extubation support
1. Respiratory Support Overview — mPaw เป็น Key Parameter

mPaw (Mean Airway Pressure) = ตัวเลขสำคัญที่สุดของ Respiratory Support
mPaw = average airway pressure over an entire respiratory cycle. mPaw ∝ FRC ∝ Oxygenation. เพิ่ม mPaw → ↑ FRC → ↑ lung recruitment → ↑ V/Q matching → ↑ PaO2. ลด mPaw → ↓ FRC → atelectasis → ↑ FiO2 requirement. Regardless of mode (CPAP, CMV, HFOV) — mPaw is what matters for oxygenation. คำนวณ: mPaw = PIP × (Ti/TCycle) + PEEP × (Te/TCycle).
2. Noninvasive Respiratory Support
2.1 Nasal Cannula (NC) Oxygen
Low-flow NC (< 1–2 L/min): effective FiO2 กำหนดโดย NC flow rate vs infant's inspiratory flow — ไม่ provide PEEP ที่ low flow; ใช้เมื่อ hypoxemia เดียว ไม่มี WOB เพิ่ม
High-flow NC (> 1 L/min, heated/humidified): ส่งแรงดัน ~1–4 cmH₂O ผ่าน nasopharynx; ช่วย lung recruitment เล็กน้อย; flush nasopharyngeal dead space → ↓ CO2
2.2 High-Flow Nasal Cannula (HFNC)
Flows ≥ 2 L/min (usually 2–8 L/min) heated/humidified; generate positive pressure in nasopharynx — magnitude unpredictable (depends on leak around cannula)

Evidence vs CPAP: HIFLOW trial (Manley 2013): HFNC non-inferior to NCPAP สำหรับ post-extubation failure; HUNTER trial (Collins 2013): HFNC similar primary outcome; comfortable, easy to use; แต่ NCPAP still preferred ใน moderate-severe RDS (more reliable pressure delivery)
Advantage: better tolerated, less nasal trauma, easier skin care than prongs; ↓ nasal septum erosion
2.3 Nasal CPAP (NCPAP)
Mechanism: continuous distending pressure → ↑ FRC → ↑ compliance → ↑ V/Q matching → ↓ WOB; ไม่ provide active inflation (ต่างจาก NIV/NIPPV)
Interfaces: short binasal prongs (most effective) > long nasopharyngeal prong (simple) > nasal mask; RAM cannula = HFNC, ไม่ใช่ CPAP
Pressure: typically 4–8 cmH₂O; RDS 5–7 cmH₂O; post-extubation 5–6 cmH₂O; ↑ to 7–8 cmH₂O ถ้า fail (ก่อน intubate)
CPAP failure signs: FiO2 > 0.4–0.5, RR > 70, apnea/bradycardia uncontrolled, respiratory acidosis pH < 7.25 → intubate + surfactant

CPAP-First Strategy — ลด BPD และ Intubation (SUPPORT, COIN, CURPAP)
SUPPORT (Finer NEJM 2010): CPAP vs surfactant in DR for 25–28 wk — CPAP non-inferior; ↓ surfactant use; non-significant ↓ death/BPD; COIN (Morley NEJM 2008): CPAP vs intubation in 25–28 wk → ↓ intubation; ↑ pneumothorax. Conclusion: CPAP first preferred ใน GA ≥ 25 wk stable; surfactant rescue if FiO2 > 0.30–0.40 within hours.
2.4 Noninvasive Ventilation (NIPPV / SiPAP)
NIPPV: delivers intermittent pressure peaks above CPAP → simulate breaths; ↑ mean airway pressure vs CPAP alone
Synchronized NIPPV (sNIPPV): synchronize with infant's own breaths → better V/T delivery; non-synchronized NIPPV = variable benefit
Evidence: Cochrane 2016 (Kirpalani): NIPPV vs CPAP post-extubation → ↓ extubation failure; less clear for primary RDS
3. Conventional Mechanical Ventilation
3.1 Anatomy ของ Ventilator Breath

Every breath characterized by: (1) Trigger: when does breath start? (time, flow, pressure); (2) Limit: what determines breath size? (pressure limit or volume target); (3) Cycle: what ends inspiration? (time, flow)
Pressure-controlled (PC): set PIP; VT varies with compliance/resistance → risk volutrauma when compliance ↑ (after surfactant)
Volume-targeted (VT): set VT target; pressure auto-adjusts to deliver VT — reduces BPD and IVH vs pressure control (Cochrane 2017 VTV meta-analysis)
3.2 Common Ventilator Modes

rows: Rarely used now; superseded by SIMV
Flow/volume/time; synchronized; VG adjusts PIP for target VT
Most common NICU mode; RDS, preterm apnea; ↓ BPD vs PC-IMV
Every breath triggered + volume-targeted; backup rate
Acute severe RDS; patient determines RR; ↑ risk hypocapnia if ↑ RR
Transition; combines benefits VT + pressure waveform

blocks.text: 3.3 Volume-Targeted Ventilation (VTV) — Gold Standard
VTV (Volume Guarantee/VG mode): set target VT 4–6 mL/kg; ventilator auto-adjusts PIP breath-by-breath to deliver target; ↓ volutrauma
Cochrane 2017 (Wheeler, Peng): VTV vs pressure control: ↓ BPD (OR 0.73), ↓ IVH grade 3/4 (OR 0.53), ↓ hypocarbia, ↓ duration MV — now recommended as standard
Pitfall: VT reading at ETT ≠ true alveolar VT (leak + circuit compliance); actual alveolar VT ≈ 3–5 mL/kg even when targeting 5–6 mL/kg at ETT
After surfactant: compliance ↑ rapidly → without VG → PIP unchanged → VT spikes → volutrauma; with VG: PIP auto-↓ → VT maintained
3.4 Initial Ventilator Settings สำหรับ RDS

rows: Match Trs; RDS = short Trs → faster OK
≥ 1Trs for RDS (Trs ~0.1s); avoid long Ti → gas trap in BPD

blocks.text: 4. Surfactant Therapy
4.1 Types ของ Surfactant

rows: High phospholipid concentration 200 mg/mL; most common

blocks.text: 4.2 Surfactant Administration Techniques
INSURE (INtubate-SURfactant-Extubate): intubate → surfactant → extubate to CPAP within 1 hr; ลด need for prolonged MV; ↓ BPD vs standard treatment (Avery 1987)

LISA (Less Invasive Surfactant Administration): thin catheter (5F feeding tube) placed in trachea under laryngoscopy via Magill forceps; infant breathing spontaneously on CPAP; surfactant delivered over 1–2 min; ไม่ต้อง intubate
MIST (Minimally Invasive Surfactant Treatment): similar to LISA using vascular catheter; some use video laryngoscopy; ไม่ใช้ Magill forceps
LISA/MIST evidence: Göpel 2011 (AMV trial), Kanmaz 2013, Mehler 2021 — ↓ need MV, ↓ BPD; Cochrane 2019 (Isayama): LISA ↓ BPD and mortality combined endpoint vs INSURE in GA 23–34 wk
Dose: poractant alfa 200 mg/kg IM dose (superior to 100 mg/kg — Rodriguez-Capote Pediatrics 2006); beractant 100 mg/kg; repeat at 6–12 hr PRN × 3 doses total

LISA/MIST คือ อนาคตของ Surfactant Delivery ใน GA ≥ 26 wk
Meta-analysis 2019 Cochrane (Isayama et al.): LISA vs INSURE → ↓ BPD or death (RR 0.75, NNT ~8) ใน GA 23–34 wk. Mechanism: avoid intubation → ↓ volutrauma from first breaths on ventilator; maintain CPAP support continuously. ใน KCMH: ถ้า GA ≥ 26 wk, stable on CPAP, FiO2 ≥ 0.30 → consider LISA ก่อน intubation.
4.3 Prophylactic vs Rescue Surfactant
Prophylactic (< 30 min after birth, intubated DR): benefit in pre-CPAP era; obsolete ถ้าใช้ CPAP-first strategy
Early rescue: FiO2 ≥ 0.30–0.40 on CPAP ≥ 5 cmH₂O within 2 hr of birth → surfactant; more evidence than late rescue
Late rescue: ≥ 2 hr of age, worsening RDS on CPAP; still effective but less impact on BPD
5. High-Frequency Ventilation — HFOV และ HFJV
5.1 Principles of HFV

HFV Concept: Small VT at Fast Frequency → Reduce Volutrauma
HFV delivers VT ≤ dead space (< 1–3 mL/kg) at very high frequency (150–900 cycles/min). Gas exchange by: (1) bulk convection (central airways); (2) asymmetric velocity profiles; (3) molecular diffusion (distal); (4) Taylor dispersion + pendelluft. CO2 elimination: ∝ VT² × frequency (DCO2 = f × VT²) — ดังนั้น amplitude (ΔP) สำคัญกว่า frequency สำหรับ CO2 removal.
5.2 HFOV — High-Frequency Oscillatory Ventilation
Delivers sinusoidal pressure wave (active push-pull) → HFOV unique feature: active expiration (unlike HFJV where expiration passive)
Parameters:
mPaw: directly adjustable = controls FRC/oxygenation; start ~2 cmH₂O higher than conventional MAP; adjust ±1–2 by CXR/SpO2
Frequency (Hz): 8–15 Hz (typical 10 Hz term, 12–15 Hz preterm); ↑ Hz → ↓ VT → ↓ CO2 removal; ↓ Hz → ↑ VT → ↑ CO2 removal
Amplitude (ΔP / Power): controls VT → controls CO2 removal; start: chest wiggle visible; ↑ amplitude → ↓ PaCO2; DCO2 = f × VT²
I:E ratio: usually fixed 1:1 or 1:2 depending on device
Indications: severe RDS failing conventional MV, PPHN (with iNO), PIE, CDH, RDS with airleak; ลด BPD ไม่ confirmed (UKOS, HIFI trials — no clear benefit vs VTV)
Pitfall: ↑ mPaw → overdistension → ↑ dead space + ↑ PVR → worse oxygenation + CO2; check CXR: diaphragm ที่ 8–9 ribs = optimal inflation
5.3 HFJV — High-Frequency Jet Ventilation
High-frequency jet: delivers short bursts of gas via jet injector (specialized ETT) at 240–660 cycles/min; expiration is PASSIVE (vs HFOV active)
Tandem with conventional ventilator: provides PEEP + backup conventional breaths (1–3/min) = "Bunnell" circuit
Parameters:
PIP (jet): controls VT → CO2 removal; start 20–22 cmH₂O
PEEP (conventional vent): controls FRC + oxygenation; HFJV unique: PEEP ↑ → FRC ↑ directly
Frequency: 420 cycles/min (7 Hz) default; ↑ → ↓ VT; ↓ → ↑ VT
Ti (jet): fixed ~0.02s (very short); passive expiration = long Te → prevent gas trapping ใน PIE
Best indications: PIE (pulmonary interstitial emphysema) = HFJV ดีที่สุด (passive expiration decompresses PIE); MAS (meconium + ball-valve), CDH, air leak syndromes, PPHN
Beware: need well-humidified gas (high velocity → dry airway → necrotizing tracheobronchitis if not humidified); HFJV CO2 drops fast — check ABG 30 min after starting
6. Oxygen Targets ใน Preterm — SpO2 91–95%

SpO2 Target 91–95% ใน Preterm < 28 wk — Evidence Base
SUPPORT (Carlo NEJM 2010): SpO2 85–89% vs 91–95% — higher target: ↓ death before discharge (19.9% vs 23.1%, p=0.04) but ↑ severe ROP. BoOST (Stenson NEJM 2013): 85–89% → ↑ mortality; 91–95% → ↑ ROP but survival benefit. COT (Schmidt JAMA 2013): 91–95% → better outcomes. Meta-analysis: SpO2 91–95% เป็น current recommended target ใน VLBW/ELBW. ตั้ง alarm: low 90%, high 96% (avoid prolonged < 90% or > 98%).
PaO2 target: 50–80 mmHg ใน preterm; > 80 mmHg = hyperoxia risk; < 50 = hypoxia
SpO2 > 95% ใน preterm = ↑ ROP risk; hyperoxia → free radical → retinal vasoconstriction → VEGF ↑ → abnormal vascularization
SpO2 < 90% = risk ↑ pulmonary vasoconstriction (PPHN worsening) + brain injury
7. Assessment และ Weaning — Extubation Readiness
7.1 Assessment ของ Respiratory Status
SpO2 monitoring: continuous; avoid chronic hyperoxia; post-DC ทารก BPD → home oximetry
Chest X-ray: 8–9 posterior ribs = optimal inflation on MV; < 8 = underinflated; > 9 = overinflated (↑ dead space + barotrauma risk)
Blood gas: target pH 7.25–7.35 ใน ventilated; permissive hypercapnia PaCO2 45–55 mmHg ใน chronic lung disease; avoid PaCO2 < 35 (→ ↓ cerebral blood flow → IVH risk)
Ventilator graphics: P-V loop: ↓ slope = ↓C; flow-volume: early terminal drop = air trapping; flow at end-exp not reaching baseline = auto-PEEP
7.2 Weaning สู่ Extubation
Wean parameters gradually: ↓ FiO2 → ↓ rate → ↓ PIP (VTV: ↑ compliance → PIP auto-decreases) → ↓ PEEP last
Extubation readiness criteria: FiO2 ≤ 0.30–0.35; MAP ≤ 7–8 cmH₂O; RR set 20–25/min; spontaneous breathing effort present; blood gas stable
Post-extubation support: CPAP 5–6 cmH₂O or HFNC 4–6 L/min; NIPPV if previous extubation failure
Caffeine: start pre-extubation if not already on; ↓ apnea → ↓ extubation failure; loading 20 mg/kg → maintenance 5–10 mg/kg/day (CAP trial)

Dexamethasone post-extubation: NOT recommended routine (risk CP); consider only intractable extubation failure with severe BPD + edema — short course 0.075 mg/kg × 3 days (DART regimen) — benefit confirmed (Doyle Cochrane 2014) but with neurodevelopmental caution
8. NICU Practical — Respiratory Troubleshooting

SpO2 ลดเฉียบพลัน บน ventilator (DOPE): Displacement (ETT), Obstruction (secretions/mucus plug), Pneumothorax, Equipment failure. ตรวจสอบ: auscultation + transillumination + CXR; hand-bag ด้วย 100% O2 ก่อน
CO2 สูงบน HFOV: ↑ amplitude (ΔP) → ↑ VT → ↑ CO2 removal; หรือ ↓ frequency → ↑ VT; check ETT position (too deep/shallow); clear secretions; ระวัง: ↑ amplitude มากเกิน → barotrauma

PIE บน conventional MV: switch to HFJV immediately — passive expiration decompresses PIE; ↓ PIP + ↑ PEEP; prone positioning ↑ drainage affected side; lateral decubitus (affected side down) ลด blood flow → ↓ perfusion → ↓ air leak extension
Post-surfactant compliance ↑ เร็ว: ถ้าใช้ VTV → PIP จะ auto-↓ (good); ถ้า pressure control → PIP unchanged → VT spikes → volutrauma. หลัง surfactant: ตรวจสอบ VT ทุก 30 min แรก; ↓ PIP ถ้าไม่ได้ใช้ VG

BPD worsening อยู่ดีๆ: (1) RSV/rhinovirus infection; (2) pulmonary edema (fluid overload); (3) pulmonary hypertension exacerbation (echo); (4) aspiration (GER); (5) secretion plugging (suction + chest PT). แยกได้ด้วย echo + CXR + virus screen
9. 5 Bedside Pearls

PEARL 1 — VTV (Volume-Targeted Ventilation) ดีกว่า Pressure Control — ตั้งแต่วันแรก
VTV (Volume Guarantee mode): ↓ BPD (OR 0.73), ↓ IVH grade 3/4 (OR 0.53), ↓ duration MV — Cochrane 2017. Mechanism: VT คงที่แม้ compliance เปลี่ยน; ลด volutrauma หลัง surfactant (PIP auto-↓). Default ควรเป็น VTV ในทุก ventilated preterm ตั้งแต่วันแรก ไม่ใช่ switch ทีหลัง.

PEARL 2 — LISA ลด BPD และ Avoid Intubation ใน GA ≥ 26 wk
Cochrane 2019 Isayama: LISA vs INSURE → ↓ BPD or death (RR 0.75, NNT ~8). Technique: Magill forceps + thin catheter (5F) + laryngoscopy; infant on CPAP throughout; surfactant ให้ช้าๆ 1–2 min. ไม่ใช่ทุก center ทำได้ — ต้องฝึก; แต่ถ้าทำได้ = preferred over INSURE ใน GA ≥ 26 wk ที่ stable.

PEARL 3 — mPaw สูงเกิน = OverDistension = Worse Oxygenation
Paradox: ↑ mPaw เกิน optimal → overdistension → ↑ dead space → ↑ PaCO2 + ↑ PVR → ↓ oxygenation. CXR monitor: 8–9 rib level = optimal. ถ้า > 9 ribs flat diaphragm = overdistended → ↓ mPaw. ↑ FiO2 โดยไม่ ↑ mPaw approach ถ้า lung already fully recruited.

PEARL 4 — HFJV = Best Mode สำหรับ PIE
PIE (Pulmonary Interstitial Emphysema): conventional MV → active expiration can worsen PIE; HFJV passive expiration + short Ti → allow PIE to decompress. เพิ่ม PEEP ↓ ใน HFJV (paradoxically); ↓ PIP; prone positioning; lateral decubitus PIE side down. HFOV active expiration → worse PIE → avoid HFOV ใน PIE.

PEARL 5 — SpO2 91–95%: ตั้ง Alarm Low 90% High 96%
SpO2 < 90% = hypoxia risk (PPHN, brain injury); > 95% = ROP risk + เพิ่ม oxidative stress. ตั้ง alarm 90–96%, target median 92–94%. BoOST+SUPPORT+COT meta-analysis: 91–95% = optimal. ห้าม accept SpO2 100% ใน preterm on O2 — เพิ่ม FiO2 ≤ 0.21 = better than 100% desaturation.
10. Evidence Update — Landmark Studies
Finer NN et al. (SUPPORT). NEJM 2010;362:1970: CPAP vs surfactant in DR for 24–28 wk — CPAP non-inferior; ↓ surfactant use; established CPAP-first strategy.
Morley CJ et al. (COIN). NEJM 2008;358:700: NCPAP vs intubation in 25–28 wk — ↓ intubation/surfactant; ↑ pneumothorax; CPAP group did better overall.
Isayama T et al. Cochrane 2019;CD011013: LISA vs INSURE in 23–34 wk — LISA ↓ BPD or death (RR 0.75, NNT ~8); preferred approach at experienced centers.
Peng W et al. Cochrane 2017;CD003440 (VTV meta-analysis): VTV vs pressure control — ↓ BPD (OR 0.73, 95%CI 0.59–0.89), ↓ IVH 3/4, ↓ duration MV; recommend VTV as standard.
Henderson-Smart DJ et al. Cochrane 2007 (HFOV vs CMV): HFOV vs CMV — modest ↓ BPD with HFOV but ↑ pneumothorax; inconsistent across trials; no clear mortality benefit.
Keszler M, Donn SM. J Perinatol 2017;37:475: HFJV for pulmonary interstitial emphysema — best evidence; passive expiration decompresses PIE; ↓ PIP.
Carlo WA et al. (SUPPORT O2). NEJM 2010;362:1959: SpO2 85–89% vs 91–95% in ELBW — higher O2: ↓ mortality (19.9% vs 23.1%) but ↑ severe ROP.
Stenson BJ et al. (BoOST). NEJM 2013;368:2094: Lower O2 saturation range 85–89% ↑ mortality vs 91–95%; confirmed higher target preferred.
Schmidt B et al. (CAP). NEJM 2006;354:2112: Caffeine for apnea of prematurity — ↓ BPD, ↓ MV duration, ↑ survival without NDI; loading 20 mg/kg, maintenance 5–10 mg/kg/day.
Kirpalani H et al. Cochrane 2016 (NIPPV): NIPPV vs CPAP post-extubation — NIPPV ↓ extubation failure (OR 0.70); less clear for primary RDS prevention.

Doyle LW et al. Cochrane 2014 (postnatal dexamethasone): Low-dose dexamethasone (DART regimen: 0.075 mg/kg) for BPD — ↓ MV time, ↑ extubation; ↑ CP risk in underpowered studies; use only for refractory cases.
11. Self-Check Questions พร้อมเฉลย
Q: mPaw คืออะไร? ทำไมจึงสำคัญกว่า PIP ใน management of oxygenation?

ตอบ: mPaw = mean airway pressure = average pressure over entire cycle = PIP × (Ti/T) + PEEP × (Te/T). สำคัญกว่า PIP เพราะ: oxygenation ∝ FRC ∝ mPaw (lung volume ถูก maintain โดย average pressure ไม่ใช่ peak pressure). ↑ mPaw via ↑ PEEP หรือ ↑ Ti ratio → ↑ FRC → ↑ V/Q matching → ↑ PaO2. ดังนั้น: ลด PIP (↓ barotrauma) แต่ maintain mPaw ด้วย PEEP = lung-protective strategy.
Q: VTV ลด BPD ผ่าน mechanism อะไร? ทำไมสำคัญโดยเฉพาะหลัง surfactant?

ตอบ: VTV (Volume Guarantee): VT target คงที่; PIP auto-↓ เมื่อ compliance ↑. หลัง surfactant: compliance ↑ เร็ว → ถ้าใช้ pressure control (PIP fixed) → VT spikes → volutrauma → lung injury → BPD. VTV prevents this — PIP auto-decrease keeps VT at 4–6 mL/kg. Cochrane 2017: ↓ BPD OR 0.73, ↓ IVH 3/4 OR 0.53, ↓ hypocarbia. Practical: after surfactant → check VT every 30 min; หรือใช้ VG mode ตั้งแต่แรก.
Q: HFOV กับ HFJV ต่างกันอย่างไร? เมื่อไหร่ prefer HFJV over HFOV?

ตอบ: HFOV: sinusoidal push-pull; active expiration; mPaw directly set; good for RDS/PPHN. HFJV: jet burst; passive expiration; PEEP controls FRC; tandem with conventional vent. Prefer HFJV when: PIE (pulmonary interstitial emphysema) — passive expiration decompresses PIE (active HFOV expiration worsens PIE); MAS with ball-valve; air leak syndromes; CDH (avoid high mPaw from HFOV). Avoid HFOV in PIE — use HFJV.
Q: CPAP-first strategy: ข้อมูลจาก SUPPORT และ COIN trial สรุปอะไร?

ตอบ: SUPPORT (Finer 2010): CPAP vs intubation/surfactant in DR ใน 24–28 wk — CPAP non-inferior; ↓ surfactant use; composite outcome death/BPD ไม่ต่าง. COIN (Morley 2008): CPAP → ↓ intubation/surfactant; ↑ pneumothorax เล็กน้อย. Meta-analysis: CPAP-first reduces both intubation and surfactant use without worsening BPD. Rescue surfactant criteria: FiO2 ≥ 0.30–0.40 on CPAP ≥ 5 cmH₂O → consider LISA. Current recommendation: CPAP-first ใน GA ≥ 25 wk ที่ stable at birth.
Q: SpO2 target ใน preterm < 28 wk: อะไรคือ evidence? ตั้ง alarm ที่ระดับไหน?

ตอบ: SUPPORT + BoOST + COT trials meta-analysis: SpO2 91–95% = ↓ mortality vs 85–89% (BoOST: 85–89% → ↑ mortality p<0.001); lower target → ↑ necrotizing enterocolitis ด้วย. Target: 91–95%; alarm: low 90%, high 96%. > 95% = ↑ ROP risk (VEGF suppression → abnormal retinal vascularization). < 90% = ↑ pulmonary vasoconstriction + brain ischemia. จำ: SpO2 100% ใน preterm on O2 = hyperoxia = harm.
Day 41 Preview

Avery Chapter 41 — Control of Breathing
พรุ่งนี้เราเสร็จสิ้น 4-วันแห่ง respiratory module ด้วย Control of Breathing — neural regulation ของ ventilatory drive: chemoreceptors (central CO2, peripheral O2), Hering-Breuer reflex, apnea of prematurity (AOP), periodic breathing. เชื่อมกับวันนี้: caffeine mechanism ผ่าน adenosine receptor antagonism → ↑ central respiratory drive = cornerstone AOP treatment. รู้ control of breathing → เข้าใจทำไม caffeine ทำงาน + ทำไม CPAP ช่วย AOP + ทำไม hypercarbia ↑ drive.
